talk_title: NK cell heterogeneity, NKG2A blockade and NK cell engager therapeutics

clinical_data.population: stage 3 non-resectable NSCLC in COAST trial

MOA_summary: The presenter defined three conserved NK cell subsets (NK1, NK2, NK3) across species, identified tumor‑associated NK3 cells (CD39+, NKG2A+, CD103+) in NSCLC that correlate with better survival, showed that anti‑NKG2A antibody (Monalizumab) can unleash their activity, and described tri‑ and tetra‑specific NK cell engagers that combine tumor antigen binding, NKp46 activation, and cytokine payloads (IL‑2 or IFN‑α) to enhance NK cytotoxicity in preclinical AML, MM, and solid tumor models.
key_findings: NK3 subset expands in NSCLC tumors and its high frequency predicts improved overall survival and better response to anti‑PD‑1 therapy.
Monalizumab (anti‑NKG2A) increases granzyme A/B expression in autologous tumor fragments, indicating functional rescue of exhausted NK cells.
Tri‑specific NK cell engagers targeting CD123 (AML) and BCMA (MM) achieved complete remissions in early‑phase AML patients, earning FDA fast‑track designation.
Tetra‑specific engagers incorporating IL‑2 or IFN‑α showed superior tumor control in MC38‑hCD20 and Raji xenograft models compared with bispecific formats.
IL‑2‑armed NK engagers induced proliferation of memory‑like NK cells in vitro, generating a CD39‑positive, cytokine‑induced memory phenotype.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: NK3 cells express high CD39, suggesting potential adenosine‑mediated suppression that may limit activity without checkpoint blockade.
Tumor‑associated NK cells are heterogeneous; NK2 (CD56bright) are scarce in tumors, indicating subset‑specific targeting challenges.

combination_strategies.rationale: Dual blockade of PD‑L1 and NKG2A to unleash both NK and CD8 T cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: PFS increased from ~30% to >90% at 12 months in COAST phase 2 NSCLC cohort

combination_strategies.rationale: Promote NK cell proliferation and memory‑like phenotype in engagers
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced NK expansion and cytotoxicity in vitro and in mouse models

open_questions: Can the NK cell engager platform achieve durable cures in solid tumors with dense stroma?
What is the optimal balance of activating vs inhibitory signals in multi‑specific NK engagers?

talk_title: Memory‑like NK cell adoptive therapy in AML and solid tumors

targets.dependency_context: CAR‑NK cells in solid tumor models
CAR‑NK targeting ovarian and pancreatic cancers

biomarkers.name: MRD
biomarkers.finding: Four of eight AML patients achieved MRD‑negative remission after memory‑like NK infusion

models.name: post‑transplant AML/MDS patients (n=8)
models.genotype_or_key_features: HLA‑mismatched donor NK cells, IL‑12/15/18 activation

models.name: mesothelin‑CAR NK in ovarian cancer mouse model
models.genotype_or_key_features: human CD20 tumor, NK cell engagers

models.name: KPC‑2 pancreatic cancer model
models.genotype_or_key_features: aggressive PDAC, IL‑12 CAR NK therapy

MOA_summary: Memory‑like NK cells are generated by overnight activation with IL‑12, IL‑15, and IL‑18, acquiring enhanced IFN‑γ production and prolonged in vivo persistence; when infused post‑transplant or after lymphodepletion, they mediate anti‑leukemic activity and, when engineered with CARs (e.g., mesothelin, CD70) and TGF‑β‑responsive IL‑12/IL‑18 payloads, they overcome tumor‑derived suppression and achieve tumor regression in solid tumor mouse models.
key_findings: Memory‑like NK cells persisted up to 200 days in some post‑transplant AML patients, far exceeding the typical 10‑12 day lifespan of conventional NK cells.
Four of eight AML patients achieved MRD‑negative complete remission after a single infusion of memory‑like NK cells.
In heavily pre‑treated head and neck cancer patients, 7 of 10 achieved disease control, including one dramatic partial response lasting ~12 months.
CAR‑NK cells armed with a TGF‑β‑responsive IL‑12/IL‑18 circuit cured >90% of mice in aggressive pancreatic and kidney cancer models without systemic toxicity.
Combining engineered tumor‑targeting bacteria expressing IL‑12 and adenosine deaminase with CAR‑NK cells doubled survival in pancreatic cancer mouse models.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Tumor‑derived TGF‑β suppresses NK function; the engineered TGF‑β sensor converts this suppressive signal into IL‑12 production, flipping inhibition into activation.
Adenosine pathway up‑regulation (CD39) on NK cells after bacterial exposure may limit efficacy, addressed by co‑expressing adenosine deaminase in the bacterial vector.

combination_strategies.rationale: Deplete Tregs via ADCC to enhance NK‑mediated anti‑tumor activity
combination_strategies.phenotype: Disease control in refractory head and neck cancer patients

combination_strategies.with_agent: Engineered E. coli expressing IL‑12 and adenosine deaminase
combination_strategies.rationale: Modulate TME by delivering cytokine and degrading immunosuppressive adenosine
combination_strategies.phenotype: Improved CAR‑NK infiltration and doubled survival in pancreatic cancer mice

open_questions: What is the optimal cytokine payload combination (IL‑12 vs IL‑18 vs others) for maximal NK activation without toxicity?
Can memory‑like NK cells achieve durable remission without subsequent allogeneic stem cell transplant?

talk_title: Logic‑gate engineered allogeneic NK cells for AML

biomarkers.name: MRD
biomarkers.finding: Majority of responders achieved MRD‑negative status

biomarkers.name: Platelet count
biomarkers.finding: Rapid recovery post‑treatment indicating limited bone marrow toxicity

MOA_summary: SENTI‑202 uses allogeneic NK cells engineered with dual activating CARs targeting CD33 and FLT3 to kill AML blasts and leukemic stem cells, combined with an inhibitory CAR against endomucin (EMCN) to protect healthy hematopoietic stem cells, and an IL‑15 transgene to enhance NK persistence, resulting in MRD‑negative remissions with minimal off‑target toxicity.
key_findings: Overall response rate of ~50% in heavily pre‑treated relapsed/refractory AML patients.
CR/CRh achieved in 30‑40% of patients with median CR duration of 7.6 months.
High MRD‑negative conversion rates observed across responders.
NK‑cell related adverse events were limited to Grade 1‑2 pyrexia; cytokine release syndrome was minimal compared with CAR‑T therapies.
Platelet and neutrophil counts recovered rapidly after lymphodepletion, indicating limited bone‑marrow toxicity.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Patients with low baseline hematopoietic stem cell counts showed MLFS responses, suggesting limited efficacy when healthy stem cells are already depleted.
Potential escape via loss of CD33/FLT3 expression remains a concern; ongoing monitoring planned.

combination_strategies.rationale: Create space for NK engraftment and reduce disease burden
combination_strategies.phenotype: Standard conditioning used in all cohorts

open_questions: Will SENTI‑202 demonstrate durable benefit without subsequent allogeneic stem cell transplant?
Can the logic‑gate platform be expanded to other AML antigens or solid tumor indications?
